userStories.title: AC refinement module
userStories.description:
Create a module that takes raw PRD acceptanceCriteria strings and refines them into concrete, testable assertions using an LLM call.

Create `src/acceptance/refinement.ts` with:

1. `refineAcceptanceCriteria(criteria: string[], context: RefinementContext): Promise<RefinedCriterion[]>` — main entry point. Takes raw AC strings from PRD, calls LLM to produce testable assertions.

2. `RefinedCriterion` type: `{ original: string; refined: string; testable: boolean; storyId: string }`. The `refined` field contains a concrete, machine-verifiable description. `testable` is false if the LLM determines the criterion cannot be automatically tested (e.g., "UX feels responsive").

3. `buildRefinementPrompt(criteria: string[], codebaseContext: string): string` — builds the LLM prompt that asks for concrete assertions.

4. `parseRefinementResponse(response: string, criteria: string[]): RefinedCriterion[]` — parses LLM JSON response back into typed objects. Must handle malformed JSON gracefully (fall back to using original criterion text as-is).

The refinement uses `adapter.complete()` from the agent registry (same pattern as classifier.ts and llm routing). Use the model tier from `config.acceptance.model` (default: "fast").

Update `src/acceptance/types.ts` with new types. Export from `src/acceptance/index.ts`.

Write tests in `test/unit/acceptance/refinement.test.ts`.
acceptanceCriteria: refineAcceptanceCriteria returns RefinedCriterion[] with original and refined fields
buildRefinementPrompt includes all criteria and codebase context in output
parseRefinementResponse handles valid JSON response correctly
parseRefinementResponse falls back to original text on malformed JSON
Criteria marked testable:false are preserved but flagged
Module uses adapter.complete() for LLM calls, not direct Bun.spawn

routing.reasoning: Standard feature with real logic (prompt building, JSON parsing, LLM integration) following established adapter pattern; requires understanding existing codebase patterns but has clear requirements and straightforward implementation path.

userStories.title: Rewrite acceptance test generator for PRD-based input
userStories.description:
Rewrite `src/acceptance/generator.ts` to generate acceptance tests from PRD `acceptanceCriteria[]` (via refined criteria) instead of from spec.md AC-N lines.

Changes:

1. Add new function `generateFromPRD(stories: UserStory[], refinedCriteria: RefinedCriterion[], options: GenerateFromPRDOptions): Promise<AcceptanceTestResult>`. This replaces the spec.md-based flow for Method 1 (direct PRD).

2. `GenerateFromPRDOptions` type: `{ featureName: string; workdir: string; codebaseContext: string; modelTier: ModelTier; modelDef: ModelDef; config: NaxConfig }`.

3. Update `buildAcceptanceTestPrompt` to accept `RefinedCriterion[]` instead of `AcceptanceCriterion[]`. The refined text should be used for test generation, not the original vague text.

4. Keep the existing `generateAcceptanceTests` function (for backward compatibility with `nax analyze`), but have it internally call the new code path.

5. Write `acceptance-refined.json` alongside `acceptance.test.ts` for audit/debugging (contains the refined criteria mapping).

Write tests in `test/unit/acceptance/generator-prd.test.ts`.

Do NOT delete the existing `parseAcceptanceCriteria` function — it is still used by the spec.md path.
acceptanceCriteria: generateFromPRD produces acceptance.test.ts content from UserStory[] and RefinedCriterion[]
Generated tests use refined criterion text, not original vague text
Each test is named with AC-N format matching the story and criterion index
Generated test file imports from bun:test (describe/test/expect)
Backward compatible: existing generateAcceptanceTests still works for spec.md path
acceptance-refined.json is written with original-to-refined mapping

routing.reasoning: Scoped refactoring with clear requirements, backward compatibility constraint, and integration of new refined-criteria input path—requires codebase understanding but no novel algorithms or architectural decisions.

userStories.title: acceptance-setup pipeline stage with RED gate
userStories.description:
Create a new pipeline stage `acceptance-setup` that runs once before story execution begins. This stage generates acceptance tests from the PRD and validates them with a RED gate.

Create `src/pipeline/stages/acceptance-setup.ts`:

1. The stage collects all `acceptanceCriteria` from all stories in `ctx.prd.userStories`.

2. Calls `refineAcceptanceCriteria()` to refine the raw criteria.

3. Calls `generateFromPRD()` to produce `acceptance.test.ts`.

4. Writes `acceptance.test.ts` to `ctx.featureDir` (e.g., `nax/features/<name>/acceptance.test.ts`).

5. RED gate: Runs `bun test <acceptance.test.ts>`. Expected behavior:
   - Exit code != 0 (tests fail) → valid RED, continue
   - Exit code == 0 (all tests pass) → tests are not testing new behavior, warn and skip acceptance

6. If `acceptance.test.ts` already exists in the feature dir (e.g., from a previous `nax analyze` run), skip generation and go directly to RED gate.

7. Store filtered criteria count in `ctx.acceptanceSetup = { totalCriteria, testableCount, redFailCount }`.

Integration:
- Add to `src/pipeline/stages/index.ts` — this stage goes in a new `preRunPipeline` array (runs once before the per-story loop, after PRD is loaded).
- Wire into the runner (`src/execution/runner.ts` or wherever the per-story loop is orchestrated) to call preRunPipeline before starting stories.

Config additions in `src/config/defaults.ts` and `src/config/schemas.ts`:
- `acceptance.refinement`: boolean (default: true) — whether to LLM-refine criteria
- `acceptance.redGate`: boolean (default: true) — whether to run RED check
- `acceptance.model`: ModelTier (default: "fast") — model tier for refinement LLM call

Rewire existing `src/pipeline/stages/acceptance.ts` (GREEN gate):
- Remove the dependency on parsing AC-N lines from spec.md
- Simply run `bun test <acceptance.test.ts>` from the feature dir (already mostly does this)
- The test file is guaranteed to exist because acceptance-setup created it

Write tests in `test/unit/pipeline/stages/acceptance-setup.test.ts`.
acceptanceCriteria: acceptance-setup stage collects criteria from all PRD stories
acceptance-setup calls refinement and generation modules
acceptance-setup writes acceptance.test.ts to feature directory
RED gate: tests that fail (exit != 0) are valid RED, stage continues
RED gate: tests that pass (exit == 0) trigger warning and skip acceptance
Skips generation if acceptance.test.ts already exists in feature dir
Config: acceptance.refinement, acceptance.redGate, acceptance.model added with defaults
Existing acceptance stage (GREEN gate) works with pre-generated test file
preRunPipeline is wired into runner before per-story loop

routing.reasoning: Multi-file integration work (7+ files: stages, runner, config, tests) with moderate logic (RED gate, criteria collection, skip logic), standard patterns, no novel algorithms or architecture decisions.

userStories.title: Integration test for RED to GREEN acceptance cycle
userStories.description:
Write an integration test that validates the full acceptance pipeline flow: generate acceptance tests from PRD criteria, verify RED gate (tests fail before implementation), and verify GREEN gate (tests pass after implementation).

Create `test/integration/acceptance/red-green-cycle.test.ts`:

1. Set up a temp directory with a minimal PRD containing 2 stories with clear acceptance criteria.

2. Mock the LLM adapter to return predictable refined criteria and test code.

3. Run acceptance-setup stage — verify:
   - acceptance.test.ts is written to the feature dir
   - RED gate detects failures (exit != 0)
   - Stage returns continue (valid RED)

4. Simulate implementation by writing stub files that make the acceptance tests pass.

5. Run acceptance stage (GREEN gate) — verify:
   - Tests now pass (exit == 0)
   - Stage returns continue

6. Test the edge case: acceptance.test.ts already exists → skip generation, still run RED gate.

7. Test the edge case: all tests already pass before implementation → warn and skip acceptance.

Use `_deps` injection pattern for Bun.spawn calls (same as other pipeline stage tests).
acceptanceCriteria: Integration test covers full RED to GREEN cycle
RED gate correctly identifies failing tests as valid RED
GREEN gate correctly identifies passing tests as feature complete
Edge case: pre-existing acceptance.test.ts skips generation
Edge case: already-passing tests trigger warning
Uses _deps injection for process spawning (no real LLM calls)
